lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨਿ ਸੀਤਾ ਆਦੀ ਡਉਰੂ ਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: jin seetaa aadee ddauroo vaae |
lines.english: Wept when he lost Sri Lanka.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਵੈ ਦਹਸਿਰੁ ਲੰਕ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: rovai dahasir lank gavaae |
lines.english: The ten-headed Raawan, who stole away Sita with the beat of his tambourine,

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੀਤਾ ਲਖਮਣੁ ਵਿਛੁੜਿ ਗਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: seetaa lakhaman vichhurr geaa |
lines.english: and separated from Sita and Lakhshman.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਵੈ ਜਨਮੇਜਾ ਖੁਇ ਗਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: rovai janamejaa khue geaa |
lines.english: Janmayjaa wept, that he had lost his way.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕੈ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਰਹਤ ਹਦੂਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: jin kai suaamee rehat hadoor |
lines.english: They were made slaves, and wept.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਵਹਿ ਪਾਂਡਵ ਭਏ ਮਜੂਰ ॥
lines.translit: roveh paanddav bhe majoor |
lines.english: The Paandavas once lived in the Presence of the Lord;

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਵਹਿ ਰਾਜੇ ਕੰਨ ਪੜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: roveh raaje kan parraae |
lines.english: The kings weep - their ears are cut;

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਕੀ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਪਾਪੀ ਭਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: ekee kaaran paapee bheaa |
lines.english: One mistake, and he became a sinner.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਵਹਿ ਸੇਖ ਮਸਾਇਕ ਪੀਰ ॥
lines.translit: roveh sekh masaaeik peer |
lines.english: The Shaykhs, Pirs and spiritual teachers weep;

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਿ ਕਾਲਿ ਮਤੁ ਲਾਗੈ ਭੀੜ ॥
lines.translit: ant kaal mat laagai bheerr |
lines.english: at the very last instant, they suffer in agony.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੋਵਹਿ ਕਿਰਪਨ ਸੰਚਹਿ ਧਨੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: roveh kirapan sancheh dhan jaae |
lines.english: The miser weeps; he has to leave behind the wealth he has gathered.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰਿ ਘਰਿ ਮਾਗਹਿ ਭੀਖਿਆ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: ghar ghar maageh bheekhiaa jaae |
lines.english: they go begging from house to house.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੰਡਿਤ ਰੋਵਹਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਗਵਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: panddit roveh giaan gavaae |
lines.english: The Pandit, the religious scholar, weeps when his learning is gone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦੁਖੀਆ ਸਭੁ ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: naanak dukheea sabh sansaar |
lines.english: O Nanak, the whole world is suffering.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੰਨੇ ਨਾਉ ਸੋਈ ਜਿਣਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: mane naau soee jin jaae |
lines.english: He alone is victorious, who believes in the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਲੀ ਰੋਵੈ ਨਾਹਿ ਭਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: baalee rovai naeh bhataar |
lines.english: The young woman weeps because she has no husband.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਮੰਨਿਆ ਮੰਨੀਐ ਬੁਝੀਐ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak maniaa maneeai bujheeai gur parasaad |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, believe in the One who is worth believing in. By Guru's Grace, he is realized. ||2||

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਉਰੀ ਕਰਮ ਨ ਲੇਖੈ ਲਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aauree karam na lekhai laae |1|
lines.english: No other action is of any account. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਪੁ ਤਪੁ ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਮੰਨਿਐ ਅਵਰਿ ਕਾਰਾ ਸਭਿ ਬਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: jap tap sabh kichh maniai avar kaaraa sabh baad |
lines.english: Meditation, austerity and everything come through belief in the Lord's Name. All other actions are useless.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਆ ਹੰਸ ਧੁਰਿ ਮੇਲੁ ਕਰਤੈ ਲਿਖਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: kaaeaa hans dhur mel karatai likh paaeaa |
lines.english: The union of the body and the soul-swan was pre-ordained by the Creator Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਣ ਗਾਵੈ ਗੁਣ ਉਚਰੈ ਗੁਣ ਮਾਹਿ ਸਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: gun gaavai gun ucharai gun maeh samaaeaa |
lines.english: Singing the Glorious Praises of the Lord, and chanting His Praises, one merges in His Glories.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੀ ਬਾਣੀ ਸਚੁ ਹੈ ਸਚੁ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sachee baanee sach hai sach mel milaaeaa |
lines.english: True is the True Word of the Guru's Bani. One unites in Union with the True Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਮਹਿ ਗੁਪਤੁ ਵਰਤਦਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: sabh meh gupat varatadaa guramukh pragattaaeaa |
lines.english: He is hidden, and yet pervading all. He is revealed to the Gurmukh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭੁ ਕਿਛੁ ਆਪੇ ਆਪਿ ਹੈ ਆਪੇ ਦੇਇ ਵਡਿਆਈ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: sabh kichh aape aap hai aape dee vaddiaaee |14|
lines.english: He Himself is everything; He Himself grants glorious greatness. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਅੰਧਾ ਹੋਇ ਕੈ ਰਤਨਾ ਪਰਖਣ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak andhaa hoe kai ratanaa parakhan jaae |
lines.english: O Nanak, the blind man may go to appraise the jewels,

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤਨਾ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣਈ ਆਵੈ ਆਪੁ ਲਖਾਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ratanaa saar na jaanee aavai aap lakhaae |1|
lines.english: but he will not know their value; he will return home after exposing his ignorance. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਗੁਣੁ ਪਲੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਮਾਣਕ ਵਣਜਹਿ ਸੇਇ ॥
lines.translit: jin gun palai naanakaa maanak vanajeh see |
lines.english: They alone purchase the gem, O Nanak, who have virtue in their purse.

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਖਰ ਤੈ ਵਣਜਾਰਿਆ ਦੁਹਾ ਰਹੀ ਸਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: vakhar tai vanajaariaa duhaa rahee samaae |
lines.english: The merchandise and the merchant are merged together.

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤਨਾ ਕੇਰੀ ਗੁਥਲੀ ਰਤਨੀ ਖੋਲੀ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: ratanaa keree guthalee ratanee kholee aae |
lines.english: The Jeweller has come, and opened up the bag of jewels.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਤਨਾ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੀ ਅੰਧੇ ਵਤਹਿ ਲੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: ratanaa saar na jaananee andhe vateh loe |2|
lines.english: Those who do not appreciate the value of the jewels, wander like blind men in the world. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਉ ਦਰਵਾਜੇ ਕਾਇਆ ਕੋਟੁ ਹੈ ਦਸਵੈ ਗੁਪਤੁ ਰਖੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: nau daravaaje kaaeaa kott hai dasavai gupat rakheejai |
lines.english: The fortress of the body has nine gates; the tenth gate is kept hidden.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਨ ਖੁਲਨੀ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਖੁਲੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: bajar kapaatt na khulanee gur sabad khuleejai |
lines.english: The rigid door is not open; only through the Word of the Guru's Shabad can it be opened.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਨਹਦ ਵਾਜੇ ਧੁਨਿ ਵਜਦੇ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁਣੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: anahad vaaje dhun vajade gur sabad suneejai |
lines.english: The unstruck sound current resounds and vibrates there. The Word of the Guru's Shabad is heard.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਮਹਿ ਏਕੁ ਵਰਤਦਾ ਜਿਨਿ ਆਪੇ ਰਚਨ ਰਚਾਈ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: sabh meh ek varatadaa jin aape rachan rachaaee |15|
lines.english: The One Lord is pervading and permeating all. He Himself created the creation. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਤੁ ਘਟ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਚਾਨਣਾ ਕਰਿ ਭਗਤਿ ਮਿਲੀਜੈ ॥
lines.translit: tit ghatt antar chaananaa kar bhagat mileejai |
lines.english: Deep within the nucleus of the heart, the Divine Light shines forth. Through devotional worship, one meets the Lord.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੇ ਕੈ ਰਾਹਿ ਦਸਿਐ ਅੰਧਾ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: andhe kai raeh dasiai andhaa hoe su jaae |
lines.english: He is truly blind, who follows the way shown by the blind man.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੇ ਏਹਿ ਨ ਆਖੀਅਨਿ ਜਿਨ ਮੁਖਿ ਲੋਇਣ ਨਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: andhe ehi na aakheean jin mukh loein naeh |
lines.english: Do not call them blind, who have no eyes in their face.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਇ ਸੁਜਾਖਾ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਸੋ ਕਿਉ ਉਝੜਿ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: hoe sujaakhaa naanakaa so kiau ujharr paae |
lines.english: O Nanak, why should the one who can see, get lost?

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੇ ਸੇਈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਖਸਮਹੁ ਘੁਥੇ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: andhe seee naanakaa khasamahu ghuthe jaeh |1|
lines.english: They alone are blind, O Nanak, who wander away from their Lord and Master. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਹਾ ਜਾਣੈ ਤੇਹੋ ਵਰਤੈ ਜੇ ਸਉ ਆਖੈ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: jehaa jaanai teho varatai je sau aakhai koe |
lines.english: He acts only as he knows, although he may be spoken to a hundred times.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬਿ ਅੰਧਾ ਜੋ ਕੀਆ ਕਰੇ ਸੁਜਾਖਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: saahib andhaa jo keea kare sujaakhaa hoe |
lines.english: One whom the Lord has made blind - the Lord can make him see again.

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਥੈ ਸੁ ਵਸਤੁ ਨ ਜਾਪਈ ਆਪੇ ਵਰਤਉ ਜਾਣਿ ॥
lines.translit: jithai su vasat na jaapee aape vartau jaan |
lines.english: Where the real thing is not seen, self-conceit prevails there - know this well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗਾਹਕੁ ਕਿਉ ਲਏ ਸਕੈ ਨ ਵਸਤੁ ਪਛਾਣਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak gaahak kiau le sakai na vasat pachhaan |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, how can the purshaser purchase the real thing, if he cannot recognize it? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਨ ਬੁਝਈ ਅੰਧਾ ਕਹੀਐ ਸੋਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: naanak hukam na bujhee andhaa kaheeai soe |3|
lines.english: O Nanak, one who does not understand the Hukam of the Lord's Command should be called blind. ||3||

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਕਿਉ ਅੰਧਾ ਆਖੀਐ ਜਿ ਹੁਕਮਹੁ ਅੰਧਾ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: so kiau andhaa aakheeai ji hukamahu andhaa hoe |
lines.english: How can someone be called blind, if he was made blind by the Lord's Command?
